name: [AT] PDEV Master Integration
description: Connects Airtable PDEV records to Bubble, prevents duplicate creation, creates missing client/project/POC records, and links all PDEV-related records together.

parameters.text: =PDEV Master integration is successful for client {{ $('Webhook').item.json.body.record["Client Name"] }}

main.node: Link Client, POC, and Project to the Requirement

record.Finance Notes:
CSA not signed yet. 
This one to follow: The client has confirmed they've read and agreed to the Placement Fee and the Two-Week Service Deposit terms.
record.Type of Client:
1.1 Client appears to be Jake McKinney / RSPUR. ❓
1.1.1 Jake’s email domain is rspur.com, and his stated email address is jake@rspur.com.
1.1.2 Follow-up Question: Confirm whether the official client name should be listed as RSPUR, Jake McKinney, or another business/entity name.

1.2 Client is seeking support with a specialized technical hiring need, not a general administrative VA placement.
1.2.1 The role requires advanced technical judgment around AI systems and engineering workflows.
1.2.2 Jake emphasized that general engineering experience is not enough; candidates must have the specific experience relevant to the work being built.

1.3 Client has already conducted a substantial independent hiring effort.
1.3.1 Jake stated he filtered approximately 600 candidates himself.
1.3.2 Jake stated he interviewed approximately 60 candidates.
1.3.3 Jake has historical candidate data, evaluation notes, and lessons learned that can materially improve Scale Virtually’s sourcing and screening process.

1.4 Client appears collaborative and willing to provide structured context to improve hiring outcomes.
1.4.1 Jake acknowledged that the initial request came with too little context.
1.4.2 Jake offered to create and send a report to give Scale Virtually a “big jumpstart.”

1.5 Client may have broader future business opportunities with Scale Virtually beyond this initial technical hire.
1.5.1 Sean requested that Jake and Sam connect so Jake could understand Scale Virtually’s broader capabilities.
1.5.2 Amanda referenced possible future arrangements such as affiliate arrangements, white-label work, or other structured delivery models.
record.Pain Points:
3.1 The largest pain point is **mismatch between general engineering ability and role-specific AI experience**. 
3.1.1 Jake previously hired experienced engineers who still failed to meet the role requirements.
3.1.2 The gap was not basic competence; it was lack of specific experience with current agent-based workflows, harnesses, and second-brain systems.
3.1.3 PDT should treat this as a precision-screening problem, not a general recruiting problem.

3.2 Scale Virtually did not initially receive a firm budget or price range. 
3.2.1 Amanda stated that this discussion was the first time she had heard the budget context.
3.2.2 Without a rate range, Scale Virtually shortlisted candidates spanning a broad $20–$60/hour range.
3.2.3 The broad rate range makes it harder to determine whether the client prefers cost savings, elite skill depth, or a balanced option.
3.2.4 ❓ Follow-up Question: Confirm whether Jake prioritizes best technical fit regardless of cost, cost-effective offshore talent, or a specific maximum hourly rate.

3.3 The original intake was underdeveloped. 
3.3.1 Jake stated the request came from “one text” between him and Sean.
3.3.2 Jake acknowledged he should provide more context.
3.3.3 Amanda stated Scale Virtually had been working from a job description Sean provided, but needed Jake’s deeper context to refine screening.

3.4 Jake has already spent significant time on hiring and likely has a low tolerance for repeated misfires. 
3.4.1 He filtered approximately 600 candidates.
3.4.2 He interviewed approximately 60 candidates.
3.4.3 He hired three engineers who did not work out.
3.4.4 PDT should avoid asking Jake to restate information that can be extracted from his prior report once received.

3.5 There is a risk of messy execution if multiple business ideas are bundled together. 
3.5.1 Amanda explicitly warned that execution becomes difficult when everything is “enmeshed together.”
3.5.2 Amanda requested that future arrangements be separated into distinct tranches of conversation.
3.5.3 Jake agreed strongly and acknowledged from experience that these arrangements can get messy.

3.6 There may be internal handoff risk between sales vision and fulfillment execution. 
3.6.1 Amanda clarified that her team is fulfillment for what Sean and Jake come up with.
3.6.2 Sam will be directing fulfillment.
3.6.3 Amanda emphasized that fulfillment needs clear direction to turn agreements into executables.
record.Tasks:
2.1 The current role is centered on a highly technical AI/engineering hire. 
2.1.1 Jake specifically referenced needing experience with **agents**.
2.1.2 Jake specifically referenced needing experience with **building harnesses**.
2.1.3 Jake specifically referenced needing experience with **building second brains**.
2.1.4 Jake referenced “all the kind of stuff that we’re doing,” indicating the work may include broader AI system-building activities that should be clarified during PEC.
2.1.5 ❓ Follow-up Question: Define what Jake means by “harnesses” in his operating context, including tools, frameworks, deliverables, and expected candidate competencies.
2.1.6 ❓ Follow-up Question: Define what Jake means by “second brains,” including whether this refers to knowledge management systems, retrieval systems, personal AI assistants, internal documentation infrastructure, or something else.

2.2 Scale Virtually shortlisted three candidates prior to this discussion. 
2.2.1 Two shortlisted candidates were from the U.S.
2.2.2 One shortlisted candidate was from the Philippines.
2.2.3 Amanda identified the Philippines-based candidate as the strongest cost-effectiveness option.
2.2.4 The shortlisted candidates ranged from approximately **$20/hour to $60/hour**.

2.3 Jake provided prior benchmark data from his own hiring attempts. 
2.3.1 Jake previously hired three American engineers.
2.3.2 Their rates ranged from approximately **$40/hour to $75/hour**.
2.3.3 They were experienced engineers, but they did not work out because they lacked the specific AI/agent/harness/second-brain experience Jake needed.
2.3.4 Jake expects global talent may provide cost arbitrage compared with U.S.-based engineering rates.
2.3.5 Jake speculated that a $60/hour global engineer may correspond to a roughly $120/hour U.S. engineer, though this was framed as his assumption rather than a confirmed benchmark. ❓
2.3.6 Follow-up Question: Confirm Jake’s target rate range for the role after reviewing the candidate pool and required skill depth.

2.4 Jake’s preferred interview process is efficient and deal-killer focused. 
2.4.1 Jake usually conducts a **15-minute first-round interview**.
2.4.2 The first interview is used to eliminate deal killers early.
2.4.3 If a candidate appears promising, Jake schedules a longer follow-up interview.
2.4.4 Sean suggested Scale Virtually could save Jake time by asking for Jake’s deal-killer format and using it during preliminary screening.

2.5 Jake agreed to proceed with the three shortlisted interviews despite the need for better role context. 
2.5.1 Jake did not want to cancel on candidates who may already be lined up.
2.5.2 Jake was open to using the interviews as a learning opportunity to determine whether Scale Virtually is on or off target.
2.5.3 Amanda framed the interviews as a calibration exercise to improve future vetting.

2.6 The next sourcing round should be rebuilt around Jake’s own candidate intelligence. 
2.6.1 Jake offered to send information from his prior hiring process.
2.6.2 Amanda requested this data so Scale Virtually can reconstruct the vetting process around the right candidate profile.
2.6.3 Jake agreed that if the first three candidates are not a fit, the second round should be stronger after Scale Virtually receives his report.
record.Proposed Solutions:
4.1 Conduct a structured PEC focused first on role calibration, then sourcing/process design. 
4.1.1 Start with Jake’s definition of success for the role.
4.1.2 Clarify required technical competencies around agents, harnesses, second-brain systems, and related AI workflows.
4.1.3 Translate Jake’s candidate evaluation history into a repeatable screening rubric.

4.2 Proceed with the three already-shortlisted interviews as a calibration mechanism. 
4.2.1 Use Jake’s feedback to determine whether Scale Virtually’s initial sourcing was aligned.
4.2.2 Capture specific reasons each candidate is or is not qualified.
4.2.3 Identify whether any disqualifying factors could have been screened before client interview.
4.2.4 Use these interviews to improve the next candidate round if none of the three are selected.

4.3 Obtain Jake’s candidate report and historical hiring data before rebuilding the sourcing strategy. 
4.3.1 Jake should send the report by replying all to Jesse’s email.
4.3.2 The report should include the people Jake hired, what he liked, what he disliked, and what criteria he used.
4.3.3 PDT should mine this report for patterns across rejected, shortlisted, interviewed, and hired candidates.
4.3.4 ❓ Follow-up Question: Confirm whether Jake’s report includes resumes, LinkedIn profiles, interview notes, scoring criteria, coding exercises, or sample work.

4.4 Build a preliminary screening process that mirrors Jake’s deal-killer interview. 
4.4.1 Ask Jake to provide his standard first-round interview questions or deal-killer categories.
4.4.2 Convert those into a Scale Virtually pre-screen form or interviewer checklist.
4.4.3 Screen candidates before client presentation so Jake only sees candidates who pass the most important filters.
4.4.4 Potential screening dimensions should include AI-agent experience, practical build history, ability to explain prior systems, familiarity with current tooling, independent problem solving, communication clarity, rate fit, and availability. ❓
4.4.5 Follow-up Question: Confirm which technical skills are mandatory versus preferred.

4.5 Create a sourcing scorecard for the role. 
4.5.1 Score technical specificity rather than generic engineering experience.
4.5.2 Include proof-of-work requirements, such as previous agent builds, automation systems, knowledge-base/second-brain implementations, or relevant demos. ❓
4.5.3 Include compensation expectations and geography as secondary filters after role fit is defined.
4.5.4 Include a clear “do not submit” profile based on Jake’s prior failed hires.

4.6 Separate this technical hiring need from any broader partnership conversations. 
4.6.1 Treat the current engineer search as its own workstream.
4.6.2 Treat potential affiliate, white-label, or broader delivery opportunities as separate workstreams.
4.6.3 Ensure each future workstream has its own agreement, scope, owner, deliverables, and handoff path.

4.7 Maintain Jesse as the operational point of contact for coordination. 
4.7.1 Jake confirmed he found Jesse’s email.
4.7.2 Jake should reply all with the additional role details.
4.7.3 Jesse should coordinate candidate interview logistics and candidate details.
record.Other Notes:
6.1 Contact information and communication flow. 
6.1.1 Jake’s best email is **jake@rspur.com**.
6.1.2 Jesse already sent Jake an email earlier that day.
6.1.3 Jake confirmed he located Jesse’s email.
6.1.4 Jake asked whether he should reply all, and Jesse confirmed yes.
6.1.5 Jake intended to set a task so he would not forget to create/send the requested context report.

6.2 Interview scheduling details. 
6.2.1 Interviews were discussed for the following day.
6.2.2 Jake is on **Mountain Time**.
6.2.3 Jake stated his morning was fully booked but he was flexible in the afternoon.
6.2.4 Jesse proposed **1:30 PM Mountain Time** the following day.
6.2.5 Jake agreed and planned to block approximately one hour.
6.2.6 One hour was considered generally sufficient for three first-round candidates, given Jake’s typical 15-minute interview format.
6.2.7 ❓ Follow-up Question: Confirm whether the three interviews occurred and document Jake’s candidate-by-candidate feedback.

6.3 Candidate-pool notes. 
6.3.1 Initial pool had a wide price range of $20–$60/hour.
6.3.2 Two candidates were U.S.-based and one was Philippines-based.
6.3.3 The Philippines-based candidate was highlighted for cost effectiveness.
6.3.4 Jake is open to interviewing candidates if they are already lined up and appear promising.

6.4 Process notes for PDT. 
6.4.1 Do not rely solely on the existing job description from Sean.
6.4.2 Treat Jake’s upcoming report as a critical input for process design.
6.4.3 Build the next sourcing round from Jake’s historical candidate data, not from assumptions about generic AI engineers.
6.4.4 Capture explicit “qualified,” “not qualified,” and “worth the rate” criteria after Jake interviews the first three candidates.

6.5 Commercial and scope notes. 
6.5.1 Jake’s expectation is that global talent may create price arbitrage compared with U.S. engineering rates.
6.5.2 However, the candidate must still have highly specific technical experience.
6.5.3 Cost effectiveness should not override role fit unless Jake explicitly confirms that tradeoff.

6.6 Future-opportunity notes. 
6.6.1 Sean wanted Jake and Sam to connect regarding what Jake is building and what Scale Virtually can support in the future.
6.6.2 Amanda recommended separating any future affiliate, white-label, or other arrangements into distinct conversations and executable workstreams.
6.6.3 Jake agreed with Amanda’s concern and recognized the risk of messy bundled arrangements.

6.7 Key open questions for PEC. ❓
6.7.1 What exact deliverables will this hire own in the first 30, 60, and 90 days?
6.7.2 Which AI tools, frameworks, languages, platforms, or environments must the candidate already know?
6.7.3 What prior project examples would prove the candidate has the right agent/harness/second-brain experience?
6.7.4 What were the specific reasons the three previously hired engineers failed?
6.7.5 What are the non-negotiable deal killers Jake wants Scale Virtually to screen before presentation?
6.7.6 What hourly rate ceiling should Scale Virtually use when sourcing?
6.7.7 Is Jake open to part-time, full-time, contract, project-based, or trial-period arrangements?
6.7.8 Who makes the final hiring decision besides Jake, if anyone?
6.7.9 What should happen if none of the three shortlisted candidates pass Jake’s first-round screen?
record.DC Recording and Summary:
# [2] Jake McKinney and Sean Connolly - June 24

Please note that the conversation includes other topics unrelated to the RFP scope. 

**[VIEW RECORDING - 60 mins (No highlights)](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb)**
## Meeting Purpose
[To align on hiring an AI engineer and explore a broader partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1116.0)
## Key Takeaways
- **[Hiring:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1269.0)**[ Scale Virtually will place an AI engineer for a $5k one-time fee, targeting a $15–20/hr rate. Jake will provide a detailed candidate report to refine the search.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1269.0)
- **[Referrals:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1311.0)**[ Jake will refer large projects beyond his scope to Scale Virtually for a 10% revenue share.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1311.0)
- **[Partnership:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1578.0)**[ A follow-up call is set for 3:30 PM MT today to strategize a larger collaboration, leveraging Jake's sales success and Scale Virtually's execution capacity.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1578.0)
- **[Process:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2180.0)**[ Amanda stressed a clear, modular approach to separate hiring from the partnership strategy, ensuring clean execution.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2180.0)
## Topics
### Jake's AI Business & Bottlenecks
- **[Rapid Growth:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=261.0)**[ Jake's business is growing quickly, turning down clients due to bandwidth constraints.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=261.0)
- **[Core Challenge:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)**[ Jake is the sole developer, leading to a "build two features, break two things" cycle. He needs a high-level engineer ($15–20/hr) with specific experience in agents and "second brain" architecture.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)
        - **[Product Suite:Productized Service (G-Brain & Hermes):](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)**[ A "done for you" service combining a portable business "second brain" (G-Brain) with an AI orchestrator (Hermes).](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)**[Pricing:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=773.0)**[ $10k minimum deposit for a 2-month build, then $2.5k–$5k/mo for maintenance.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=773.0)
    - **[Sales Process:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=795.0)**[ Automated discovery → AI-generated scope of work with a Stripe link for a $5k deposit.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=795.0)
        - **[Leadforge:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)**[ A lead-gen service with ~250 bots scraping real estate data.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)**[Challenge:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2787.0)**[ The SaaS UI is bloated and unstable from constant custom feature additions.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2787.0)
    - **[Pivot:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2820.0)**[ Considering a simpler lead-delivery service to focus on the core data engine.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2820.0)
### Proposed Partnership & Hiring Plan
    - **[Strategic Partnership:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1116.0)**[ Sean proposed a collaboration to leverage Jake's sales success and Scale Virtually's execution capacity.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1116.0)**[Referrals:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1137.0)**[ Jake will refer large projects (e.g., the telehealth platform) for a 10% revenue share.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1137.0)
    - **[Scaling:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1520.0)**[ Scale Virtually can provide a team to help Jake scale his own productized services.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1520.0)
    - **[Hiring Plan:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1606.0)[Fee:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1269.0)**[ $5,000 one-time placement fee.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1269.0)
        1. **[Process:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1727.0)[Refine Search:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1759.0)**[ Jake will provide a detailed report on his experience filtering 600+ candidates to help Scale Virtually target the right skills.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1759.0)
        2. **[Initial Interviews:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1955.0)**[ Jake will interview 3 pre-screened candidates tomorrow at 1:30 PM MT.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1955.0)
    1. **[Vetting:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2051.0)**[ Scale Virtually will use Jake's "deal killer" criteria for future preliminary screening.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=2051.0)
### Technical Deep Dive (Jake & Sam)
- **[Development Workflow:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)**[ Jake uses Claude Code with 7+ terminals open, pushing code daily.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
- **[Token Spend:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=3380.0)**[ ~$1,000/month across multiple plans (Claude, Olama, OpenRouter). Jake's goal is a $5,000/month burn rate, which he sees as a KPI for high activity.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=3380.0)
- **[Key Insight:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=3120.0)**[ Jake realized clients are acting as testers. He plans to build an AI-driven testing suite to catch bugs before deployment, enabling him to maintain a fast development pace.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=3120.0)
## Next Steps
    - **[Jake:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1870.0)**[Send candidate report to Jesylou (`jake@rspur.com`).](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1870.0)
    - [Interview 3 candidates: **June 25, 1:30 PM MT**.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1955.0)
    - **[Paul:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1470.0)**[Draft and send the 10% referral affiliate agreement.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1470.0)
    - **[Sean:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1578.0)**[Call Jake to strategize the larger partnership: **June 24, 3:30 PM MT**.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1578.0)
    - **[Jesylou:](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1955.0)**[Send calendar invite for the candidate interviews.](https://fathom.video/share/7zmqB1cNAro9ux-KnBsQQ_sodiU_GrFb?tab=summary&timestamp=1955.0)
